# FRAMEWORK PDF CAPTIONS — bgd-flooding 2025-04-25
# framework_doc: https://www.unocha.org/attachments/b9e1bf90-a96b-490f-bfc7-a31e98de669d/Bangladesh%20AA%20Framework%20-%20Flood%20-%20April%202025%20-%20FINAL%20.pdf
# pages: 14 · model: sonnet · via: claude headless
# Auto-generated descriptions of the PDF's visuals (maps/charts/tables). PUBLIC.
------------------------------------------------------------
--- page 1 ---
Cover and Executive Summary: "Bangladesh Monsoon Floods – 2025 Version" Anticipatory Action Framework. Approved by RC 7 April 2025, endorsed by USG/ERC 25 April 2025. Photo of a woman (AA recipient, 2024) with cows. Key point: OCHA-coordinated AA framework for monsoon river floods, first activated 2020 and again 2024; 2025 version expands geographic coverage and strengthens coordination with government/humanitarian partners.

--- page 2 ---
Table of contents and Introduction. No figure. Key point: framework covers risk/hazard, triggers, activation protocol, delivery activities, financing (CERF + co-financing), learning/M&E, and annexes (trigger messages, priority intervention areas).

--- page 3 ---
Two trigger definition tables side by side: Jamuna River Basin vs. Padma River Basin.

Readiness trigger:
- Jamuna: water level at Bahadurabad gauging station forecast by FFWC 5-day model to exceed "Danger Level" + 0.85 m for ≥3 consecutive days AND probabilistic forecasts (GloFAS) show >50% likelihood of crossing 1-in-5-year return period.
- Padma: water level at Baruria transit near Goalando station forecast by GloFAS model with 12–15-day lead time to be >50% likely to cross the 1-in-5-year return period.

Key point: readiness trigger uses FFWC deterministic model (Jamuna) or GloFAS probabilistic model (Padma) with different lead times and thresholds.

--- page 4 ---
Action trigger definitions (continuation of trigger tables) and Activation protocol table.

Action trigger:
- Jamuna: water level at Bahadurabad forecast by FFWC 5-day model to cross "Danger Level" + 0.85 m AND GloFAS/RIMES probabilistic forecasts with longer lead times show sustained/increasing trend of discharge ≥1-in-5-year return period, starting from when danger level is forecast to be crossed.
- Padma: water level at Mawa station forecast by FFWC 5-day model to cross "Danger Level" + 0.5 m AND probabilistic forecasts with 3–15-day lead show sustained/increasing trend at Baruria transit ≥1-in-5-year return period for ≥3 consecutive days.

Activation protocol table — Who / What / How / When:
- OCHA-led forecast monitoring team ("Hassan"): monitors GloFAS + FFWC daily; sends alert to big group when 15-day readiness trigger reached. From June.
- OCHA ("Hassan"): confirms 15-day readiness trigger → communicates status to RC/RCO and other partners (Annex). As triggers reached.
- CERF: when readiness trigger is issued, CERF will send approval letters. When trigger reached.
- RC/RCO: meeting with WFP, CERF, BDRC, technical working group on anticipatory action; organises immediately after readiness/action trigger reached. As applicable.

Key point: two-step activation (readiness → action) with FFWC + GloFAS/RIMES cross-check; OCHA coordinates communication.

--- page 5 ---
Improvements since 2020 and Challenges (narrative text, no figure).
Key improvements: GloFAS updated from v2.2 to v3.1 (improved readiness trigger performance — fewer false activations and missed events); 15-day readiness lead time added to framework.
Key challenges: FFWC water level forecasts have negative bias at longer lead times/high water levels (underprediction → risk of missed triggers); collaboration with FFWC ongoing. River basins still not fully covered by coordinated AA framework.

--- page 6 ---
Map: "Bangladesh Coordinated Anticipatory Action for Monsoon River Flooding."
Target districts highlighted (dark teal): Jamuna basin — Bogra, Jamalpur, Gaibandha, Kurigram; Padma basin — Madaripur, Manikganj, Shariatpur. Legend shows "Target districts" (dark) vs. "Covered districts" (lighter). Total 38 Upazilas across 7 districts. Key point: geographic expansion vs. 2023 edition adds Padma basin districts; coverage determined by flood risk, vulnerability, and implementation capacity.

--- page 7 ---
Narrative text: joint targeting/common beneficiary database, joint approach to early warning, localization and government collaboration, challenges. No figure.
Key point: common beneficiary database is a major challenge; FAO SMS early warning system (government-linked) complements AA; RC/RCO and BDRC/government partnerships central; data-sharing agreement still needed for full database integration.

--- page 8 ---
Financing overview table — Pre-arranged funding by agency, Jamuna and Padma basins:

| Agency | Jamuna CERF | Co-fund | Other Financed | Other | Padma Other Financed | Other |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| FAO | $400,000 | – | – | – | $195,640 | – |
| UNICEF | $300,000 | – | – | – | $277,000 | – |
| UNFPA | $630,000 | – | – | – | – | – |
| BDRC | $3,000,000 | $1,400,000 | $1,200,000 | $600,000 | – | – |
| CARE | – | – | $250,000 | – | – | – |
| Concern Worldwide | – | – | $37,100 | – | – | – |
| Start Network | – | – | $115,000 | – | – | – |
| Save the Children | – | – | $20,695 | – | – | – |
| **Total** | **$4,330,000** | **$1,400,000** | **$1,672,640** | **$600,000** | | |

Grand total approx. $9,005,685. CERF funds disbursed once trigger is reached (no pre-approval needed once framework is endorsed). Key point: BDRC receives the largest allocation ($3M CERF Jamuna + $1.4M co-fund); total framework budget ~$9M.

--- page 9 ---
Challenges (financing) and The Learning sections. Narrative text, no figure.
Key points: start-up/"build costs" not funded by CERF; collective anticipatory action requires investment. Learning agenda covers timing, joint targeting, early warning content, localization, actions per agency, effectiveness, and participation rates. Independent evaluation planned; M&E questionnaires to be harmonised across agencies.

--- page 10 ---
Final provisions — Advocacy and public communication. Narrative text only, no figure.
Key point: agencies coordinate messaging under RC leadership; joint press release and social media amplification on activation; visuals/impact stories shared with ad hoc committee.

--- page 11 ---
Annex: Overview of framework — two summary tables for Jamuna River Basin and Padma River Basin.

Jamuna districts: Bogra, Gaibandha, Jamalpur, Kurigram.
Padma districts: Madaripur, Manikganj, Shariatpur.

Agency activity/people/financing rows (Jamuna):
- WFP: High-nutrient food distribution, Waterproof storage silos. People Targeted ~5,000. CERF $3,000,000. Co-fund $1,400,000. Other $600,000.
- FAO: Cash support. CERF $400,000.
- UNICEF: Jerricans, water purification, emergency treatment plants. CERF $300,000.
- UNFPA: Dignity kits, menstrual health, antenatal support, baby kits, reproductive health, mobile camps. CERF $630,000.

Padma financing shown in Other Financed column: FAO $195,640, UNICEF $277,000.
Key point: one-page framework summary with triggers, activities, people counts, and financing per agency/basin.

--- page 12 ---
Annex: DRAFT trigger messages — email template, readiness trigger.
Subject: "Bangladesh – Readiness trigger reached for [Jamuna/Padma]"

[JAMUNA] trigger text: discharge at Bahadurabad gauging station forecast by GloFAS for ≥3 consecutive days; GloFAS max 15-day lead; >50% likely to cross 1-in-5-year return period water discharge value of 100,000 m³/s on [DATE]; remain above it [DATE].

[PADMA] trigger text: water discharge at Baruria transit near Goalando station forecast over 3 consecutive days by GloFAS 5-day lead time model with 12–15-day lead; >50% likely to cross 1-in-5-year return period.

Key point: standardised trigger notification email template with basin-specific threshold language.

--- page 13 ---
Annex: DRAFT trigger messages — action trigger email template (continuation).
Subject: "Anticipatory Action – Action trigger reached for [Jamuna/Padma]"

[JAMUNA] action trigger: water level at Bahadurabad forecast by FFWC 5-day lead deterministic model to cross "Danger Level" + 0.85 m for ≥3 consecutive days from [DATE]; AND probabilistic forecasts (GloFAS/RIMES) show sustained or increasing trend of discharge ≥1-in-5-year return period for ≥3 consecutive days beginning from when danger level is forecast to be crossed.

[PADMA] action trigger: water level at Mawa station forecast by FFWC 5-day lead deterministic model to cross "Danger Level" + 0.5 m; AND probabilistic forecasts (GloFAS/RIMES) with 3–15-day lead show sustained or increasing trend at Baruria transit for ≥3 consecutive days beginning when danger level is forecast to be crossed.

Key point: action trigger email template specifying both deterministic (FFWC) and probabilistic (GloFAS/RIMES) conditions for each basin.

--- page 14 ---
Annex: Priority intervention areas for coordinated anticipatory action — table listing River basin, District, Upazila, Adm3 Pcode.

Padma basin districts and upazilas (sample): Madaripur (Shibchar BD305487, Madaripur Sadar BD305678, etc.), Manikganj (Shibalaya BD305678, Daulatpur BD305614, etc.), Shariatpur (Bhedarganj BD306614, etc.).
Jamuna basin districts and upazilas: Bogra (Sariakandi BD501081, Dhunat BD501061, Sonatala BD501091), Jamalpur (Islampur BD453915, Dewanganj BD453910, Baksiganj BD453907, Sarishabari BD453985, Melandaha BD453961, Jamalpur Sadar BD453958, Madarganj BD453958), Kurigram (Nageshwari BD554861, Bhurungamari BD554906, Ulipur BD554994, Rajibhat BD554977, Char Rajibpur BD554901, Chilmari BD554909, Kurigram Sadar BD554862), Sirajganj (Kazipur BD508850, Sirajganj Sadar BD508857, Royganj BD508861,Ullah Para BD508894).

Total: 38 Upazilas listed with BD admin-3 pcodes across 7 districts (Padma + Jamuna basins). Key point: definitive geographic scope with admin-3 pcode identifiers for targeting and data linkage.
